Inflation Signal
← Back to dashboard

Inflation Signal

Broad U.S. inflation signal from price-level dynamics.

As of 2026-04-30

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given the inflation_signal z-score of 0.003 and a scale value of 20.4, this setup aligns with Transition-biased price paths and Indeterminate volatility, where the dominant risk is Regime shift, not trend continuation.
  • The signal is currently classified as COOL by the data provider, though its quantitative level indicates a directionless structural turning point (1).
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Mixed Signals; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes. Signal Stability Assessment: Deteriorating; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Composite z-scores between -0.25 and +0.25 (currently 0.003) define a TRANSITION regime, indicating high regime-flip risk (1).
  • Values are interpreted relative to a 10-year rolling trend to quantify aggregate latent pressure (1).
  • Falling z-scores imply a disinflationary impulse or demand deceleration, suggesting potential for monetary easing (1).
  • Dataset: Inflation Signal (Latest observation: 2026-04-30).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the sharp decline in the composite z-score from 0.346 (Feb 2026) to 0.003 (April 2026), moving deep into the transition zone (1).
  • Momentum indicates a deteriorating inflationary state, though it remains above the -0.50 threshold required for a confirmed "COOL" regime by methodology standards.
  • Internal Conflict: The provided data labels 0.003 as "COOL," while the methodology defines values between -0.25 and +0.25 as "TRANSITION" (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A reversal in z-score momentum exceeding +0.25 would negate the current disinflationary transition.
  • The signal demonstrates low stability, having shifted regime labels twice in the last four months.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant; risks balanced due to the directionless nature of the transition zone.
  • Upside Risk: A re-acceleration of 3-month annualized CPI above the YoY rate, triggering a regime flip toward "HOT."
  • Downside Risk: A continued decline below -0.50, confirming a structural disinflationary trend.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months), reflecting the 10-year rolling window and multi-month momentum rules.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Low, justified by the high regime-flip risk and internal classification conflicts.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the broader US Inflation & Price Dynamics macro theme (2).
  • Economic mechanism: The signal implies a disinflationary impulse, which typically serves as a tailwind for fixed income and a warning of cyclical demand slowdown (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • This signal should be cross-referenced with M2 liquidity and Copper demand to distinguish between cost-push and demand-pull dynamics.

Regime Context:

  • Newly entered the transition zone after a period of relative stability in the upper neutral range.
  • Direction of change: Weakening towards the disinflationary boundary.

Model Limitations:

  • The signal is sensitive to CPI revisions and seasonality adjustments (1).
  • TIPS liquidity bias during market stress can distort the market-implied components (1).

Data & References:

  • Inflation Signal (Latest: 2026-04-30).
  • Dominant influence: Composite z-score (0.003) and 0-100 scale (20.40).
  • Data timeliness cannot be assessed (Data Freshness: Unknown).
  • External verification: 10Y Breakeven Inflation Rates and M2 Money Supply.
